name: Cima del Joar via the historic trail of Álava — circular route through the Entzia Special Conservation Zone
name_original: Cima del Joar por el sendero histórico de Álava — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación de Entzia
region_intro: Hiking around Campezo / Kanpezu offers diverse natural landscapes within the historical territory of Álava in the Basque Country. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the Codés Range and Sierra de Lokiz, alongside the Ega River. Hikers encounter extensive beech forests, gall oak woodlands, and dramatic limestone ridges, particularly within the Izki Natural Park. Best hiking trails around Campezo / Kanpezu The most popular hiking route is Cima del Joar via the historic trail of Álava — circular route through the Entzia Special Conservation Zone, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 6 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to Joar Peak, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Joar Summit (1,414 m) – Ioar loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This challenging trail leads to the highest point in the Codés mountain range. Local hikers also love the Aguaque Waterfall loop from Antoñana, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through natural beauty to a waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around Campezo / Kanpezu is defined by mountainous terrain, limestone formations, and dense beech forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy waterfall loops to challenging summit ascents. The routes in Campezo / Kanpezu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Campezo / Kanpezu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: There are currently 38 hiking trails recorded in Campezo / Kanpezu. These include 10 easy routes, 19 moderate paths, and 9 challenging hikes, offering options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: Hikers in Campezo / Kanpezu will encounter a diverse range of landscapes, from extensive beech forests and gall oak woodlands to dramatic limestone ridges and formations. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the Codés Range and Sierra de Lokiz, with the Ega River adding to its natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Izki_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, in particular, offers a wide array of distinct natural features.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous walk. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10515565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguaque Waterfall loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) long and leads through natural beauty to a waterfall. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oteo Waterfall – Sabando Waterfall loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.9 miles (16 km) long and also features waterfalls.

Q: Where can I find waterfalls along the hiking trails?
A: The region is home to several picturesque waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10515565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguaque Waterfall loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt; for an easy walk. For a slightly longer, moderate hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oteo Waterfall – Sabando Waterfall loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the broader area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4387004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabando Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: The routes in Campezo / Kanpezu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from shaded beech forests to impressive cliffs, and the well-marked paths that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, the region offers challenging ascents to prominent peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050662165/cima-del-joar-via-the-historic-trail-of-alava-circular-route-through-the-entzia-special-conservation-zone" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cima del Joar via the historic trail of Álava&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.4 miles (16.8 km) circular route that ascends to Joar Peak, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6974462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m) – Ioar loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path to the highest point in the Codés mountain range.

Q: Are there any unique attractions or landmarks to explore along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several unique attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leorza-Cicujano Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, part of an old greenway, has been restored and decorated with 45 murals depicting local life. You can also visit the charming medieval town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz de Campezo&lt;/a&gt; or the nearby village of Antoñana, a National Monument Complex. For stunning views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1254551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sleeping Lion Peak (Lapoblación Castle)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: Yes, the area features several excellent circular routes. For a challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050662165/cima-del-joar-via-the-historic-trail-of-alava-circular-route-through-the-entzia-special-conservation-zone" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cima del Joar via the historic trail of Álava&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. For a moderate hike with scenic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5344221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Gorge Viewpoint – Soila loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt; offers a 6.8 miles (11 km) circular path.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: Campezo / Kanpezu offers great hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring brings lush greenery and flowing waterfalls, while autumn transforms the extensive beech and oak forests into a spectacle of colors. Summer is ideal for higher altitude hikes, and even winter can be beautiful, especially in the lower valleys, though some higher routes may require appropriate gear for snow or ice.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting in the region?
A: Yes, the diverse ecosystems of Campezo / Kanpezu, especially within the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Izki_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, provide excellent opportunities for wildlife spotting. The rugged limestone formations, such as Costalera, are significant for rupicolous birds. Hikers might also encounter various forest animals in the extensive beech and oak woodlands.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning panoramic views. The ascent to Joar Peak (1,417 m) offers rewarding vistas, as does Pico León Dormido (Castillo de Lapoblación) above 1,300 meters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5344221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Gorge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via a moderate loop from Antoñana, provides scenic vistas of the Izki Gorge itself.

Mountain refuge very well taken care of... and may it continue like this for a long time, for the good of all. A good place to shelter, in a mountain with very harsh winter days.

Simply amazing postcards from the top of Joar at more than 1400 meters above sea level. To get here you can take alternative paths of greater or lesser degree of difficulty depending on your preference, in any case the route is very picturesque.
